county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1833 from Cooper. County seat: Versailles.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Versailles holds land records from 1833. Morgan County is on the Osage River and Lake of the Ozarks. Morgan County Historical Society in Versailles has genealogical files. Check Cooper County for pre-1833 records. Lake of the Ozarks (1931) flooded communities — check Corps of Engineers relocation records. Strong Southern settlement from Kentucky and Tennessee. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1833

source_profiles.source_name: Morgan County Deed Records -- Recorder of Deeds, Versailles
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1833 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation (1833)

source_profiles.source_name: Morgan County Probate Records -- Probate Court, Versailles
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1833. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Morgan County Marriage Records -- Recorder of Deeds / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1833. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Morgan County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1833-1950 for Morgan. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.source_name: Morgan County Original Land Entry Records -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Morgan County. Missouri is a public land state — BLM GLO records document first transfer of land from federal government. Some areas have earlier French/Spanish colonial grants.

source_profiles.source_name: Morgan County Enslaved Persons Records -- Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.repository: Missouri State Archives, Jefferson City / Probate Court, Versailles
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, slave schedules, bills of sale, manumission records, and probate records for Morgan. Missouri was a slave state until 1865.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax records and estate inventories generally well preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Morgan County Tax Records -- Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists and personal property assessments from 1833. List heads of household, property, livestock, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Useful as census substitutes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Generally well preserved at Missouri State Archives

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1812 from Original district. County seat: New Madrid. Missouri Bootheel (southeastern lowlands) — cotton agriculture region. French colonial heritage — area settled under French and Spanish governance before Louisiana Purchase (1803).
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in New Madrid holds records from 1812 with gaps due to the 1811-1812 New Madrid Earthquakes, the most powerful earthquakes in recorded US history east of the Rockies. The earthquakes destroyed buildings, changed the course of the Mississippi River, and caused massive population displacement. New Madrid Historical Museum has surviving records and genealogical files. Spanish colonial records for this area survive at Missouri State Archives. French colonial heritage. New Madrid 'earthquake certificates' (land claims) at NARA document displaced settlers. Bootheel cotton agriculture. FamilySearch microfilm covers available records. Strong French and Southern settlement.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Missouri State Archives microfilm and digitized records for New Madrid from colonial era

source_profiles.source_name: New Madrid County Deed Records -- Recorder of Deeds, New Madrid

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation (1812)

source_profiles.source_name: New Madrid County Probate Records -- Probate Court, New Madrid
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1812. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1812. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1820-1950 for New Madrid. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: French colonial court records, Spanish colonial land grants, parish registers, and administrative records for the New Madrid area. Louisiana Purchase (1803) transferred governance to the US.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1838 from Barry. County seat: Neosho.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Neosho holds land records from 1838. Newton County is in the Tri-State lead and zinc mining district. Neosho served briefly as Confederate capital of Missouri in 1861. George Washington Carver National Monument is in Newton County — Carver was born enslaved here in the 1860s. Newton County Historical Society has genealogical files. Significant mining immigration (Cornish, Welsh, Italian). Check Barry County for pre-1838 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Camp Crowder (WWII) brought military families to the area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1838

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Andrew. County seat: Maryville. Part of the Platte Purchase (1836) — last major land acquisition from Native Americans in Missouri.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Maryville holds land records from 1845. Nodaway County is in northwest Missouri. Northwest Missouri State University in Maryville has local history collections in the B.D. Owens Library. Nodaway County Historical Society maintains genealogical files. Strong Midwestern farming heritage. Check Andrew County for pre-1845 records. Nodaway River valley settlement. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. BLM GLO records document original land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1845

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Ripley. County seat: Alton. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Alton holds records restarting after Civil War guerrilla destruction that burned the courthouse and destroyed most early records. For pre-war records, check Ripley County (parent), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO land patents. Oregon County is one of the most remote and sparsely populated counties in Missouri — deep Ozark terrain. Eleven Point River and Greer Spring area. Strong Southern Appalachian settlement heritage. Oregon County Historical Society in Alton has limited post-war files. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war records. Church records are critical supplements.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1845

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Gasconade. County seat: Linn. Missouri German Belt — significant German immigrant settlement from the 1830s-1860s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Linn holds land records from 1841. Osage County is the heart of Missouri's German Catholic settlement — nearly every community has a German Catholic church with records from the 1840s. Osage County was named for the Osage Nation. Rich Hill and Westphalia are notable German settlements. St. Joseph Catholic Church records in Westphalia and other parishes are essential. Osage County Historical Society has family files. Check Gasconade County for pre-1841 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri wine country. German-language records require translation skills.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1841

county_metadata.record_losses: Civil War destruction and 1868 fire; nearly all early records lost
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Taney. County seat: Gainesville. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement. Burned county: Civil War destruction and 1868 fire; nearly all early records lost
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Gainesville holds records restarting after Civil War destruction and the 1868 fire that destroyed nearly all early records. For pre-fire research, check Taney County (parent), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO land patents. Ozark County is among the most remote counties in Missouri. North Fork of the White River area. Strong Southern Appalachian settlement heritage — subsistence farming and timber. Ozark County Historical Society in Gainesville has limited post-fire files. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire records. Church records (Baptist, Church of Christ) are essential supplements.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1841

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1851 from New Madrid. County seat: Caruthersville. Missouri Bootheel (southeastern lowlands) — cotton agriculture region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Caruthersville holds land records from 1851. Pemiscot County is in the Bootheel lowlands with major cotton and soybean agriculture. Pemiscot County Historical Society has genealogical files. Significant African American population — sharecropping and tenant farming records. Caruthersville was an important Mississippi River town. Check New Madrid County for pre-1851 records. Levee district and drainage records are important for land history. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Strong Southern settlement from Tennessee, Mississippi, and Arkansas.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1820 from Ste. Genevieve. County seat: Perryville. French colonial heritage — area settled under French and Spanish governance before Louisiana Purchase (1803). Missouri German Belt — significant German immigrant settlement from the 1830s-1860s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Perryville holds land records from 1820. Perry County has exceptional German heritage — the Saxon Lutheran immigration of 1839 settled here, founding the Lutheran Church-Missouri Synod. Trinity Lutheran Church (Altenburg) and Concordia Seminary records are nationally significant for Lutheran genealogy. Perry County Historical Society in Perryville has genealogical files. Saxon Lutheran Memorial in Frohna. French colonial heritage through Ste. Genevieve connection. Catholic parish records also from the 1820s. FamilySearch microfilm is extensive. Check Ste. Genevieve for pre-1820 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Missouri State Archives microfilm and digitized records for Perry from colonial era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1833 from Cooper, Saline. County seat: Sedalia.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Sedalia holds land records from 1833. Pettis County was a major railroad center — Sedalia was headquarters of the MKT (Katy) Railroad. Sedalia Public Library has local history and genealogy collections. Pettis County Historical Society maintains genealogical files. Scott Joplin composed 'Maple Leaf Rag' in Sedalia (1899). Missouri State Fair in Sedalia since 1901. Strong German immigrant community. Significant enslaved persons records pre-1865 — Cooper and Saline (parent counties) were slaveholding. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate.

source_profiles.source_name: Pettis County Tax Records -- Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists and personal property assessments from 1833. List heads of household, property, livestock, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Useful as census substitutes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Generally well preserved at Missouri State Archives
